name: bedtime_natvent_continuation
description: Nat-vent active at bedtime with outdoor below sleep target — fan continues past bedtime, cycles down to sleep_heat floor overnight
notes: Occupant outcome: On a warm evening the occupant opens windows; outdoor air (69°F) is cooler than indoors (73°F) and below the sleep target. At bedtime CA normally deactivates the fan — but with nat-vent continuation (Issue #370), the fan keeps running through the night. The fan cycles on/off around sleep_heat (64°F) using nat_vent_temperature_check()'s sleep-window branch. The occupant's home cools naturally while they sleep, without the AC ever turning on.

Scenario effectiveness: If the nat-vent continuation gate is deleted from handle_bedtime(), bedtime unconditionally calls _deactivate_fan() and nat_vent_bedtime_continue is never emitted — the assertion at 22:30 fails. The old Priority 0 sleep-ceiling exit (nat_vent_sleep_ceiling_reached) has been REMOVED (Issue #371) — cycling now handles fan on/off via nat_vent_temperature_check(); the session only ends via Priority 2 (indoor ≤ comfort_heat) or outdoor-warmth exits.

Two-way alignment: handle_bedtime() evaluates sleep band before fan deactivation; gate condition is _natural_vent_active AND _fan_active AND NOT _fan_override_active AND outdoor < sleep_band.ceiling. nat_vent_temperature_check() sleep-window branch uses sleep_heat+hysteresis as the cycling target (off at sleep_heat, on at sleep_heat+2×hysteresis). Priority 0 (nat_vent_sleep_ceiling_reached) has been deleted from check_natural_vent_conditions().

User outcome if test fails: Occupant loses free cooling past bedtime — CA shuts the fan off exactly at 22:30, running the AC unnecessarily to bring indoor to sleep temp instead of letting natural ventilation do the work for free.

Config note: sleep_cool=72 is explicitly set below. sleep_heat=64 provides the cycling floor. comfort_cool=74 gives headroom so the outdoor temp (69°F) is below both comfort_cool and sleep_cool, satisfying the nat-vent continuation gate. sleep_time and wake_time are required — _in_sleep_window() returns False without them, so the sleep-window cycling branch would never activate. fan_mode=hvac is required — _activate_fan() returns early when fan_mode==disabled and never sets _fan_active=True, so the bedtime gate (which requires _fan_active) would always fail. The HVAC fan archetype uses set_fan_mode on the climate entity, which the headless harness supports without a separate fan_entity.

events.note: Evening classification — warm day, comfort band armed at [68/74].
Evening: indoor 73°F, outdoor 69°F — 4°F cooler than indoor, well below sleep target 72°F. Nat-vent conditions met.
Window opens. Nat-vent activates: outdoor 69°F < indoor 73°F, indoor 73°F > comfort_heat 68°F, outdoor 69°F < comfort_cool+delta 77°F.

Bedtime fires. sleep band = [64/72]. Nat-vent continuation gate: _natural_vent_active=True, _fan_active=True, NOT _fan_override_active, outdoor 69°F < sleep_cool 72°F → gate passes. Fan stays running; emit nat_vent_bedtime_continue instead of deactivating. Sleep band still programmed.
Indoor has cooled to 64°F — at sleep_heat (the cycling floor). Injects temperature so the subsequent nat_vent_temperature_check event has the correct indoor reading.

Periodic cycling check fires (every 5 min in production). nat_vent_temperature_check() sleep-window branch: target=65 (sleep_heat+hysteresis=64+1), off_threshold=64. indoor(64) ≤ off_threshold(64) → fan cycles off, session stays active (_natural_vent_active=True). No session-end event. If indoor warms above on_threshold(66), fan resumes.

assertions.reason: Window opens with outdoor 69°F < indoor 73°F and outdoor below threshold 77°F — nat-vent activates, fan on, _natural_vent_active=True.

Bedtime fires with outdoor 69°F < sleep_cool 72°F — continuation gate passes, nat_vent_bedtime_continue event emitted, fan preserved. If gate is deleted, bedtime calls _deactivate_fan() instead and this event is never emitted.

Indoor reaches sleep_heat floor (64°F) at 01:00 in sleep window. nat_vent_temperature_check() sleep-window cycling: target=65 (sleep_heat+hysteresis), off_threshold=64. Fan cycles off, session stays active. The old nat_vent_sleep_ceiling_reached (Priority 0) has been removed — cycling now handles this transition.

verdict.summary: Nat-vent continues past bedtime (Issue #370) when outdoor is below sleep target; cycles down to sleep_heat floor rather than stopping at sleep_cool
verdict.observed_behavior: sensor_open → natural_ventilation (20:30) → bedtime fires → nat_vent_bedtime_continue (22:30, fan stays running) → indoor cools to 64°F → nat_vent_fan_off (01:00, fan pauses at sleep floor, session remains active)
verdict.expected_behavior: Occupant opens windows at 20:30; CA activates nat-vent. At 22:30 bedtime, CA checks outdoor (69°F) vs sleep target (72°F) — outdoor is cooler, so fan keeps running. Overnight the fan cycles on/off around sleep_heat (64°F) using the sleep-window cycling branch. At 01:00 the home has cooled to the sleep floor; fan pauses cycling. AC never ran overnight — maximum free cooling, minimum energy use.
